name: The Escape
description: A protagonist trapped by physical, social, or psychological confinement engineers liberation.

nodes.label: Confinement
nodes.definition: The protagonist is trapped — in a prison, an oppressive system, a controlling relationship, or a psychological cage. The walls are established as real and difficult to breach.
entry_conditions: Audience sees the nature and severity of the confinement
exit_conditions: The protagonist decides that escape is necessary and begins to assess the possibility
failure_modes: Confinement is too comfortable — no urgency to escape
The walls are never made concrete enough for the audience to feel them
signals_in_text: Other trapped characters who have given up

nodes.label: Spark of Hope
nodes.definition: Something reveals that escape might be possible — a weakness in the system, an ally, a piece of knowledge, or a change in circumstances.
entry_conditions: A new element enters that changes the calculus
exit_conditions: The protagonist begins to believe escape is possible and starts planning
typical_variants: Discovery of a flaw in the prison
Arrival of a new ally or prisoner with key knowledge
An internal realization that the confinement is not as absolute as it seemed
failure_modes: Hope is so faint the audience doesn't believe it
Hope is so obvious the confinement seems incompetent
signals_in_text: New information that changes the protagonist's posture

nodes.label: Planning the Escape
nodes.definition: The protagonist develops a plan — gathering resources, recruiting co-conspirators, studying routines, and identifying the critical moments where the system is vulnerable.
entry_conditions: The spark of hope has been identified
The protagonist has the will to act
exit_conditions: The plan is ready to execute (or as ready as it can be)
typical_variants: Gradual undermining of the confinement over time
Plan developed in partnership with others
failure_modes: Planning is too thorough — no remaining uncertainty
Plan is never shown — the escape seems improvised without justification

nodes.label: Setbacks and Near-Discovery
nodes.definition: The escape plan is threatened by complications — increased surveillance, betrayal, a change in routine, or the protagonist's own mistakes. Each setback tests resolve and forces adaptation.
entry_conditions: The system or antagonist creates obstacles
exit_conditions: The setbacks are overcome or worked around; the plan is adjusted and still viable
typical_variants: A co-conspirator is caught or turns traitor
An unexpected change invalidates part of the plan
failure_modes: Setbacks are too minor to create tension
Setbacks are so severe the plan should be impossible but continues anyway

nodes.label: Point of No Return
nodes.definition: The protagonist commits to executing the escape. After this moment, failure means not just continued confinement but worse punishment. There is no going back to quiet endurance.
entry_conditions: The plan is viable despite setbacks
A triggering event forces the protagonist to act now or never
typical_variants: Discovery is imminent and delay means capture
The protagonist's mental state demands action now
failure_modes: No sense that commitment raises the stakes
The protagonist can still abort without consequence

nodes.label: The Escape Attempt
nodes.definition: The plan is executed. Things go wrong, improvisation is required, and the outcome hangs in the balance. This is the story's climactic sequence.
entry_conditions: The point of no return is passed
exit_conditions: The protagonist either breaks free or is captured
typical_variants: Escape that requires sacrifice of a co-conspirator
failure_modes: Escape goes exactly as planned — no tension
Escape depends entirely on luck rather than the protagonist's capability or growth

nodes.label: Freedom
nodes.definition: The protagonist has escaped. Freedom is real but not free — it carries the cost of what was endured, what was sacrificed, and the knowledge that confinement exists.
exit_conditions: A new equilibrium is established outside the confinement
typical_variants: Freedom that reveals the outside world has its own constraints
failure_modes: Freedom is purely celebratory with no acknowledgment of cost
Freedom scene is rushed or absent
Character shows no lasting effect of the confinement
signals_in_text: Looking back at what was left behind
Adjusting to a world without walls

edges.label: Hope Appears
edges.preconditions: Confinement is fully established; a new element changes the possibility
edges.effects_on_stakes: The protagonist now has something to lose beyond endurance — they have hope
edges.effects_on_character: Shifts from passive survival to active assessment
edges.common_alternatives: Hope comes from outside; hope comes from within (a realization)
edges.anti_patterns: Hope is implausible given the established confinement

edges.label: Begins Planning
edges.preconditions: The spark of hope is identified; the protagonist decides to act on it
edges.effects_on_stakes: Planning creates both hope and risk — discovery means punishment
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist becomes strategic, purposeful, and secretive
edges.common_alternatives: Planning is solo; planning requires recruiting allies
edges.anti_patterns: Plan is fully formed instantly with no process of development

edges.label: Obstacles Arise
edges.preconditions: The plan is in development; the system or antagonist creates complications
edges.effects_on_stakes: Each setback raises the risk and narrows the margin for error
edges.effects_on_character: Resolve and ingenuity are tested; trust among co-conspirators is strained
edges.common_alternatives: Setbacks come from external changes; setbacks come from internal betrayal
edges.anti_patterns: Setbacks are cosmetic and don't actually threaten the plan

edges.label: Now or Never
edges.preconditions: Setbacks have been managed; a trigger demands immediate action
edges.effects_on_stakes: Failure now means not just continued confinement but worse punishment
edges.effects_on_character: The protagonist commits fully — fear is overridden by determination
edges.common_alternatives: Trigger is an external deadline; trigger is imminent discovery
edges.anti_patterns: Commitment carries no additional risk beyond what already existed

edges.label: Executes the Plan
edges.preconditions: The point of no return is passed; action begins
edges.effects_on_stakes: Maximum — freedom or capture, life or death
edges.effects_on_character: Everything the protagonist has become during confinement is tested
edges.common_alternatives: Execution is methodical; execution is desperate improvisation
edges.anti_patterns: Escape goes exactly as planned with no complications

edges.label: Breaks Free
edges.effects_on_stakes: External confinement is over; internal stakes remain
edges.effects_on_character: The protagonist is free but permanently marked by the experience
edges.common_alternatives: Freedom is immediate; freedom requires a final sprint or sacrifice
edges.anti_patterns: Freedom is costless and purely joyful
